name: 陳儀
objective: This course is specifically designed for international students with Chinese proficiency at the A1 level. Centered around everyday life situations, it introduces Taiwanese society, culture, and related topics through text reading and thematic discussions, providing students with a basic understanding of these areas. The course includes assignments and in-class activities that offer ample opportunities to practice listening, speaking, reading, and writing in Chinese, aiming to enhance students’ overall language proficiency.
schedule:
Week 1  Getting to Know the Campus
Week 2  Getting to Know Taipei
Week 3  Taiwanese Festivals
Week 4  Taiwanese Culture and Religious
Week 5  Activity: Walking and Observing Taipei
Week 6  Taiwanese Scenery
Week 7  Project Discussion: Topic 
Week 8  Taiwanese Music
Week 9  Taiwanese Cuisine and Food
Week 10  Activity: Walking and Observing Taipei 
Week 11  Project Discussion: Content
Week 12  Taiwanese Film
Week 13  Group Project: Independent Research Report 1
Week 14  Group Project: Independent Research Report 2
Week 15  Group Project: Independent Research Report 3
Week 16  Constitution Day
Week 17  New Year's Day
Week 18  Semester Reflection and Sharing
scorePolicy:
Class Participation and Assignment: 50%
Activity Participation: 20%
Group Project: 30%
materials: Kohara, Yuriko, et al. Chinese Made Easy for Japanese Learners (????? 台華語). New Taipei City: Chihkuan Culture, 2020.
consultation: If you have any questions, please email: chenyi@mail.ntut.edu.tw.
課程是否導入AI: ● 鼓勵學生使用生成式 AI 工具（Encourage students to use generative AI tools）
remarks: 1. Course Corresponding Indicators: 4. Quality EducationThe corresponding course content for Indicator 4, "Education for sustainable development and global citizenship," includes teaching topics from Weeks 2-17, which focus on the Chinese language and culture.2. The course topics in Week 13-15, titled "Independent Research Report" are related to the "Exhortation" stage of the innovative character education 6E teaching method. Through group activities, the course aims to enhance character practice.
